claim: Supabase is fundamentally PostgreSQL with PGVector extension, enabling it to serve both as a SQL database and vector store for RAG without needing separate tools
actionable: Use Supabase for both conversation history (SQL) and vector retrieval (PGVector) in the same database, simplifying your AI stack compared to separate Postgres + Qdrant
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Supabase can be self-hosted entirely locally with Docker using their official docker-compose setup, avoiding cloud dependency for AI agents
actionable: Clone Supabase's Docker repository, merge their docker-compose with your AI stack's compose file, and configure environment variables for a fully local database
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Supabase has multiple Docker containers for different services (Studio, Kong, Auth, Realtime); combining multiple docker-compose files requires Python script to merge them into one network
actionable: Use a Python script to merge Supabase's docker-compose with your AI stack's compose file and ensure all containers run on the same Docker network for inter-service communication
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: When containers reference the database inside docker-compose, use 'db' (the container name); when n8n references Supabase's vector store from outside, use 'host.docker.internal:8000'
actionable: Reference Supabase database within containers by container name (db:5432), but from n8n to Supabase vector store use host.docker.internal to escape the container network
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Environment variables for local Supabase require JWT secret, anonymous key, and service role key; these can be generated using Supabase's official self-hosting documentation tool
actionable: Use Supabase's self-hosting docs to generate JWT and API keys for your environment variables; don't hardcode them
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: File triggers in n8n workflow can watch a local folder and feed files into vector database; the '/data/shared' folder in n8n maps to your host machine for easy file transfers
actionable: Set up n8n file trigger watching '/data/shared', place files in your host's shared folder, and have the workflow chunk and embed them into Supabase vector store
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Vector size in Supabase PGVector table must match your embedding model; nomic-embed-text from Ollama uses 768 dimensions
actionable: Set your Supabase vector table size (e.g., 768) to match your embedding model's output dimensions, not a generic size
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Restarting local AI stack (updating env vars, pulling latest containers) requires running the start service script; data persists across restarts in Docker volumes
actionable: Modify .env, then run start_services script again to restart containers; your n8n workflows, Supabase data, and Ollama models are preserved
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Cleaning vectors from Supabase before re-inserting updated file content prevents duplicate or stale vectors from old versions
actionable: In n8n file trigger workflows, use metadata filters to delete existing vectors for the file before inserting updated vectors
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents

claim: Local AI stack (Supabase + n8n + Ollama) enables fully private RAG where sensitive documents never leave your machine
actionable: Use local Supabase for proprietary document RAG; keep sensitive data off cloud APIs
source_title: Run Supabase 100% LOCALLY for Your AI Agents
